name: How much should I budget for a kitchen remodel in Pacifica or Daly City?
headline: How much should I budget for a kitchen remodel in Pacifica or Daly City?

mainEntity.name: How much should I budget for a kitchen remodel in Pacifica or Daly City?
mainEntity.text: How much should I budget for a kitchen remodel in Pacifica or Daly City?

acceptedAnswer.text: A realistic budget for a kitchen remodel in Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, or Half Moon Bay depends mainly on how much you change the layout and the level of finishes you choose. In the Bay Area, a **full kitchen remodel** that updates cabinets, countertops, appliances, flooring, lighting, and some layout changes often falls roughly in the **$45,000 to $85,000** range. This assumes a typical-sized kitchen, mid-range materials, and licensed labor. If you move plumbing, add new circuits, or remove walls, you’ll be closer to the higher end. Lighter “refresh” projects—like painting cabinets, new countertops, and a few fixtures—can sometimes be done for **$15,000 to $30,000**, but won’t change the underlying layout. Common budget pitfalls include: - Underestimating electrical and plumbing work, especially in older Coastside homes. - Forgetting permit and inspection costs when you move walls, add circuits, or change plumbing. - Blowing the budget on finishes (high-end stone, custom cabinets) and then having to cut corners on lighting or ventilation. A practical approach is to list your must-haves (safety, wiring, layout fixes) before your nice-to-haves (premium counters, high-end appliances). Add a 10–20% contingency for surprises behind walls. Talk to at least two licensed contractors and insist on itemized estimates so you can see where the money is going before you commit.
